_provenance.why: BLAKE2s is the one crypto primitive ts_tunnel uses that has no direct KAT elsewhere. ts_tunnel uses it in two widths/forms: (1) 32-byte output (blake2::Blake2s256) backs the WireGuard Noise_IKpsk2 transcript hash, the mac1/mac2 MAC-KEY derivations, and the HKDF chaining-key expansion (ts_tunnel/src/handshake.rs imports blake2::{Blake2s256, Digest}; ts_tunnel/src/macs.rs; ts_tka SigHash); (2) a 16-byte-output keyed MAC (type CookieMac = Blake2sMac<U16>, ts_tunnel/src/macs.rs:18) is the actual mac1/mac2 cookie MAC written to the packet trailer, keyed via new_from_slice (src/macs.rs:91) so a <32-byte key (the live 16-byte cookie key) is padded correctly. The Wycheproof project ships no BLAKE2 set, so the 32-byte vectors are sourced from the canonical BLAKE2 reference KAT; the 16-byte cookie-MAC vectors are produced by the same blake2 crate (the dep ts_tunnel ships) and cross-checked against Python hashlib.
_provenance.convention: Canonical BLAKE2 KAT: an input of length n is the bytes 00 01 02 ... (n-1). Keyed vectors use a fixed key 00 01 02 ... (keylen-1): KEY32 = 00..1f (32 bytes), KEY16 = 00..0f (16 bytes). Keyed BLAKE2s is a MAC construction parameterized by the key (blake2::Blake2sMac256 / Blake2sMac<U16> via KeyInit / new_from_slice), NOT a hash over key||input.
anchors: cookie  Blake2sMac<U16>("", key=00..0f short) = af4e5d3bb230e9ff9096ca6f7c501f76 (exercises new_from_slice short-key padding)
_provenance.coverage: Both BLAKE2s flavors ts_tunnel uses are covered: the 32-byte hash/transcript path (unkeyed Blake2s256 + 32-byte-keyed Blake2sMac256), AND the 16-byte cookie-MAC path (Blake2sMac<U16>) including a SHORT (<32-byte) key that drives the new_from_slice padding the live cookie MAC relies on (src/macs.rs:91). The 32-byte vectors are in `vectors`; the 16-byte cookie-MAC vectors are in `mac16_vectors`.
_provenance.verification: Every 32-byte out value was produced by RustCrypto's blake2 crate v0.10.6 (the proven dependency ts_tunnel ships) over the canonical inputs, and independently cross-checked against testvectors/blake2-kat.json and against an unrelated implementation (Python hashlib.blake2s). The 16-byte Blake2sMac<U16> out values (not present in the canonical 32-byte-only KAT file) were produced by the same blake2 crate (keyed via new_from_slice + finalize_fixed, mirroring src/macs.rs) and independently cross-checked against Python hashlib.blake2s(digest_size=16, key=..), matching byte-for-byte. The Rust assertions live in ts_tunnel/tests/blake2s_kat.rs (blake2s256_reference_kat for the 32-byte path, blake2s_mac16_kat for the 16-byte cookie-MAC path; vectors embedded there as arrays to avoid a JSON-parsing dev-dependency; this file is the committed provenance copy).

mac16_vectors.note: SHORT 16-byte key (KEY16), empty input — exercises new_from_slice short-key padding (the cookie-MAC path, src/macs.rs:91)
SHORT 16-byte key (KEY16), input 0x616263 ("abc") — short-key padding path
SHORT 16-byte key (KEY16), 32-byte input — short-key padding path
